Delight. Delight. Delight. Happy Holidays!

author: @1justpara
text:
I am a crafter of many things. I carve leather for belts, bags, purses etc, and I make cards and pictures with parchment but my main craft is crochet.  Clothes, toys, miniatures, you name it, I make it.  
I'm not 'bragging', I only tell you this because what you have said is true.  
I didn't become as skilled as I am overnight.  It took many years of putting into practice what I call ..  THE THREE P'S......  Practice, Patience and Perseverance.  Without them you will fail.  My one regret is that I didn't take time to learn woodwork as I would love to create dolls houses like the one you've just made.  It's wonderful and your daughter and granddaughter will be so proud of you.  Thank you for sharing.

author: @misamsung6191
text:
I built a Barbie doll house for my daughter and for some reason we kept it even when we moved houses.  The Grand Daughter saw it in my workshop and wanted to play with it.  It got renoed and is now in pride of place in our living room.  BUT my partner said that I should build one for Grand Daughter. I did and now she has a three story Barbie's Scandinavian house (I stole the design from Ikea).  I do hope that the doll house and the barn shaped toy box that I built for her remain in the family and are passed down.  

BTW for all my projects I use laminated pine boards.  They are cheaper and a bit more dimensionally stable and come in a variety of widths and lengths.

author: @stl7694
text: Was I was young I wanted to make my sister a doll house.  We got a pine three shelf bookcase from a garage sale and put shelf dividers in it with door holes cut out in each one.  Some paint mis-tints and scraps of wallpaper finished it out.  At her age she loved it.  When she got older she used it as a bookcase.  Then, She gave it to her daughter who is now using to keep children’s books in for her son.  I think that whole project cost maybe 10 dollars.  Including the bookcase.

author: @SvenRötters
text:
If you know how it works, you can usually do it yourself. The rest is money, practice and failure.
The only downsite is the hording. Once you are settled in the MacGuyver mindset, you always find stuff you can use for 'later'. 😁

author: @grendel1960a
text:
when I was small my Dad made me a castle, he made  my sister a dolls house, the next christmas I got the outer bailey for the castle, with a drawbridge, my sister got a zoo. after that he built me some ramp sections and a bridge, following that with more different bridge sections, over the years these toys were passed down to nieces and nephews, and then to their children. I have made castles too, and an ark for my daughter - of course this was in the days when a sheet of plywood was relatively inexpensive. I have bought wardrobes and cupboards - just for the timber, some having plywood, one having nice mahogany parts. currently I have a washing machine to dismantle for parts (it stopped working 2 days ago and got replaced) I also pick up any non working printers for disassembly, they are full of gears shafts, bolts and small motors, the metal and plastic frames go for recycling.
My mum was a teacher before i was born, teaching pottery, and dressmaking, so to go with the wooden toys Dad made she would sew felt animals, some made from old felt hats.
its always good fun recycling old stuff (or repairing it where possible) and then you have cheap materials for all your maker projects.

The grand daughter saw it in my work room and wanted to play with it.  Once they left It was renoed and now sits in pride of place in our living room.  I put it on wheels so she could spin it around when she comes over and wants to play in it.

author: @KavanOBrien
text: Where does one buy ethanol from and how much is it for 5 litres and how long does it last? Sorry not talking about dolls house, hope the house is warmer than mine even dolls get cold.

replies.author: @ScoreGuru123
replies.text: I bought 4 x 5 litre tubs ( Methanol ) off eBay for £35.  If you're using it for the heater in video 1702, it lasts about an hour. I've no idea how Rob got his to last 4 hours, I copied his design and one hour is all I get.  It would work out cheaper to get a calor gas heater and use that if you want to suppliment your gch, or use for emergency

replies.author: @KavanOBrien
replies.text: @ScoreGuru123 thanks, but with calor gas you have to buy the fire then the bottle not cheap unless I’m wrong not been in this position before.

replies.author: @johnmcfadden9336
replies.text: It’s a very expensive fuel . Robs suggestion of heating the body and not the space could be a better solution car seat heaters and heating pads that can be sown into clothing is probably the cheapest way to go in addition infrared heating right next to the body has health benefits too

replies.author: @KavanOBrien
